issues.claim: engineering analog at the operational layer for cosmic chain: action → yajña → rain → food → beings
issues.objection: The verse's metaphysical reach (yajña as Vedic ritual / Prajāpati / brahman / akṣara) genuinely exceeds what the engineering layer can carry. The analog is honestly tagged STRETCHED and the engineering body explicitly registers this with 'pale shadow / silhouette' framing. A careful Sanskritist would still flag that a casual reader might miss the 'analog only' caveat; the explicit STRETCHED tag handles this. Acceptable.

notes: 3.14 — Sanskrit terminology preserved without misrepresentation. The verse's key term (yajñaḥ karma-samudbhavaḥ) is anchored in the engineering body. Both the Advaita and Vishishtadvaita readings are cited correctly per the source pack's commentaries section. The disagreement among translators on the key word is captured at the source level, not flattened in the engineering layer. No mistranslated tokens; tradition labels (Advaita / Vishishtadvaita) used precisely. Pass.
